name: First Community Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank in Milton, Wisconsin serving the area for over 100 years with traditional banking services and online tools.
description_long:
First Community Bank has operated continuously since its founding as Farmers Bank on July 17, 1911, in Milton, Wisconsin. The institution survived the Great Depression and underwent significant ownership and operational changes, including being acquired by First Community Bancshares in 1986 and rebranding to First Community Bank in 1989. The bank has modernized through multiple facility expansions and remodels, most recently in 2013, and approached $100 million in assets by 2016.

The bank offers traditional retail banking services including mortgage lending with online application capability, mobile banking via iOS and Android apps, and online account management. They provide financial calculators for mortgages, vehicles, college planning, credit cards, and retirement. The bank emphasizes customer service through personal bankers available for account inquiries and maintains FDIC insurance coverage on deposits.

First Community Bank distinguishes itself through its deep community roots and long operational history in Milton. The institution emphasizes cybersecurity awareness, ID theft prevention resources, and customer service excellence. Their physical footprint has expanded to include three storefronts on Merchant Row, and they actively promote both digital banking convenience and in-person service relationships.

As a small regional community bank, First Community Bank serves a localized market and appears to offer standard banking products rather than specialized consumer finance solutions. The website provides limited detail about specific product features, rates, or fees, requiring direct contact with personal bankers for detailed service information. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online account management and digital banking
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of the U.S. Government
Over 105 years of continuous community banking history and stability
Mobile banking apps available on both Android and iOS platforms
Online mortgage application capability for convenience
Multiple financial calculators available (mortgage, vehicle, college, credit card, retirement)
Physical branch presence with expanded storefronts and modernized facilities
Dual phone lines for customer service (1-800-261-8053 and 1-608-868-7644)
cons: Limited geographic reach as a small regional bank focused only on Milton, Wisconsin area
Website provides minimal information about specific products, rates, fees, or terms
No clear indication of competitive loan rates or account features compared to larger institutions
Minimal detail about business banking services despite having business-focused resources
Email communication explicitly discouraged due to security concerns with no secure alternative mentioned
best_for: Milton, Wisconsin residents seeking local banking relationships and community-based service
Customers preferring traditional brick-and-mortar banking with modern digital conveniences
Individuals interested in banks with deep historical roots and established community stability
diagnosis: First Community Bank is best for Milton area residents seeking traditional FDIC-insured banking with local service and modern digital options. The main caveat is the limited product transparency on their website—prospective customers will need to contact personal bankers directly to compare rates, fees, and specific product terms against competing institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
